content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
William H. Madison 
Jones Hall Hill & White 
July 25, 1988 
Four Embarcadero Center, Suite 1950 
San Francisco, CA 
94111 
Dear Mr. Madison: 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-274 
You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions 
of the Political Reform Act of 1974 (the Act) .17 
QUESTION 
Does a $10,000 contribution to an initiative measure 
obligate you to file a statement under the Act? 
CONCLUSION 
If you made a $10,000 contribution between January 1, 1988, 
and June 30, 1988, to an established committee supporting or 
opposing an initiative, you are considered a "major donor" 
committee and must file the enclosed form 461. 
This form is 
due no later than August 1, 1988.~ 
If you made expenditures for communications which support 
or oppose the initiative without consulting or coordinating 
your expenditures with any political committee, you must file 
the form 461 'as an independent expenditure committee. 
FACTS 
You have made a $10,000 contribution to the Solano County 
Quality of Life Initiative. 
You have not indicated whether the 
contribution was to an existing committee, nor whether you made 
any other political contributions in California during the 
period January 1, 1988 through June 30, 1988. 
liGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California~ode 
of Regulations. 
~The filing deadline is July 31, which is a Sunday. 
Therefore, the deadline is extended to the next regular 
business day, August 1. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660 


Madison, William H. 
Page Two 
ANALYSIS 
section 82015 defines a "contribution" as 
a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by 
a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment 
except to the extent that full and adequate consideration 
is received unless it is clear from the surrounding 
circumstances that it is not made for political purposes. 
An expenditure made at the behest of a candidate, committee 
or elected officer is a contribution to the candidate, 
committee or elected officer unless full and adequate 
consideration is received for making the expenditure. 
Section 82013 defines three different kinds of committees. 
A committee means any person or combination of persons who 
directly or indirectly does any of the following: 
(a) Receives contributions totaling one thousand dollars or 
more in a calendar year; 
(b) Makes independent expenditures totaling one thousand 
dollars or more in a calendar year; or 
(c) Makes contributions totaling ten thousand dollars or 
more in a calendar year to or at the behest of candidates or 
committees. 
ThUS, if your contribution of $10,000 was made directly to 
or at the behest of a committee, you qualify as a major donor 
committee. 
(Section 82013(c).) 
section 84200 (b) requires 
that major donor committees file campaign statements no later 
than July 31 for the period January 1 through June 30, and 
January 31 for the period July 1 through December 31 of the 
previous year. 
The same is true if you qualify as an 
independent expenditure committee. 
(Section 82013 (b).) 
Section 84215 indicates where campaign reports must be 
filed. 
If you made no other contributions to California state 
and local candidates or -committees, you must file the original 
and one copy-of the statement with the Solano County Clerk's 
office. 
However, if other contributions were made outside 
Solano County, you are required to file the original and one 
copy of the statement with the Secretary of State, two copies 
with the Registrar of voters of San Francisco, and two copies 
with the Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles county, in addition 
to two copies with the Solano County Clerk. 
All political contributions made to California state and 
local candidates and committees for the period January 1 
through June 30, 1988, must be reported on the same form 461. 


. { 
Madison, William H. 
Page Three 
Enclosed is the FPPC Information Manual on Campaign 
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act for Major 
Donor Committees and Independent Expenditure Committees. 
If you have any additional questions, please contact me at 
(916) 322-5662 . 
by: 
Sincerely, 
Dian~ M. 
'iffith~-
2ft 
,""", ~ 
f¥,J;-., / 
fC tA > 
, 
!ilV',.. 
~(die ,{.\ 
en. B aaten-Moen 
Polit'cal Reform Consultant 


A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION 
ATTORNEYS AT LAW 
"d ,f 
t3 
CHARLES F. ADAMS 
STEPHEN R. CASALEOGIO 
ANDREW C. HALL. JR. 
KENNETH I. JONES 
PHILIP NELSON LEE 
WILLIAM H. MADISON 
BRIAN D. QUINT 
PAUL J. THIMMIO 
SHARON STANTON WHITE 
F2.ir P/11iH~81 PrRctices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, California 95814 
July 11, 1988 
FOUR EMBARCADERO CENTER 
SUITE 1950 
SAN FRANCISCO 94111 
(415) 391 5780 
AUTOMATIC TELECOPIER 
(415) 391 1">784 
(415) 956 6:108 
RE: 
Contribution to Solano County Quality of Life Initiative 
Gentlemen: 
We have been advised by our client, the City of Fairfield, that our contribution to 
the captioned initiative measure in the amount of $10,000 may require us to file a 
• statement with the Fair Political Practices Cominission. The City was advised by the 
firm of Townsend & Co., 1717 High Street, Suite B, Sacramento, California, 95814, that 
a filing may be necessary by reason of the size of our contribution. Please advise if a 
filing is required, and if so, we would appreciate your forwarding the necessary forms on 
to us so that we may complete them. 
Very truly yours, 
/J~ 
William H. Madison 
cc: Andy Hall 
WHM:pch:M7022
